name: Fix log --follow hang with stopped daemon

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Fix log --follow hang with stopped daemon is complete and correct

scope: BUG: wolfcastle log --follow with stopped daemon hangs forever. Spec says show last session and exit. See docs/specs/2026-03-21T18-00Z-log-command-design.md for the expected behavior. Fix the --follow code path to detect that the daemon is not running and exit cleanly after rendering the last session instead of blocking indefinitely.
success_criteria: wolfcastle log --follow with a stopped daemon exits cleanly after rendering the last session
wolfcastle log --follow with a daemon that dies mid-session detects the exit and stops within a few seconds
No behavioral change when daemon is running: follow mode still streams live output

breadcrumbs.text: Created 2 children: follow-no-op-when-stopped (2 tasks), follow-reader-daemon-exit-detection (3 tasks). Ordering: the no-op guard runs first because it's the simpler fix addressing the primary reported bug (explicit --follow with stopped daemon). The daemon-exit detection leaf runs second because it addresses the subtler scenario where the daemon dies mid-session, and it builds on the same understanding of the follow code path. Both leaves are independent and could theoretically run in parallel, but the sequential order keeps the diff reviewable.
